title: Official macro provider-order decision dossier

attemptOrder: eligible stale panel return with background refresh
terminal stop on absent or disabled FRED credential
Treasury batch for missing DGS2 DGS10 DGS30
skip matching FRED DGS after usable Treasury rows
remaining FRED series in fixed sequential order
consumer projection with exact observation truth

currentPolicy.microCacheEligibility: non-empty, structurally valid, non-stale observations only
currentPolicy.stalePanelTreatment: eligible stale display fallback, never promoted to fresh source authority
currentPolicy.credentialActivationBehavior: absent or disabled FRED credential stops remaining cold network fanout
currentPolicy.ordinaryVixFailureBehavior: transient or empty VIXCLS does not prevent the Treasury attempt
currentPolicy.isolationViolationBehavior: propagates to the outer cache/fallback boundary and is not treated as provider miss

treasury.role: direct US Treasury daily par-yield publisher

fredDgs.role: FRED relay of official Treasury daily-yield series

truthRules: reject stale calendar rows from the official macro aggregate
do not treat a proxy as official
do not treat synthetic values as real observations
do not treat fixture transport as production or live evidence
do not treat not checked as ready
nominal authority does not make an unusable row usable

protectedNodes.disagreement: test expects FRED DGS before Treasury
protectedNodes.requiredT568Assertion: Treasury precedes FRED DGS fallback after Treasury timeout

protectedNodes.disagreement: test expects all critical FRED DGS attempts before Treasury
protectedNodes.requiredT568Assertion: VIX remains first, Treasury is DGS primary, and FRED DGS is fallback

protectedNodes.disagreement: test expects the FRED sequence before Treasury
protectedNodes.requiredT568Assertion: bounded Treasury slot leaves aggregate budget for remaining FRED fallback series

protectedNodes.disagreement: test expects FRED DGS calls even though successful Treasury rows intentionally suppress them
protectedNodes.requiredT568Assertion: the three consumers reuse one observation set and successful Treasury DGS suppresses duplicate FRED DGS calls

history.subject: fix(market-overview): protect FRED macro budget
history.effect: moved implementation and the four expectations toward FRED-first behavior

history.subject: fix(market-overview): repair provider regression baseline
history.effect: restored Treasury before the remaining FRED loop without reconciling the four expectations

history.subject: fix(provider): preserve isolation through injected seams
history.effect: tightened injected transport identity and no-live enforcement without changing provider order

history.subject: fix(history): reconcile managed cache readiness contracts
history.effect: changed cache and fallback truth without changing provider order

history.subject: fix(integration): reconcile provider and cache truth
history.effect: changed provider error import ownership without changing provider order
allocated the exact four residual failures to T568 and required this decision

options.name: Treasury-first
options.definition: After cache lookup, use Treasury first for overlapping DGS observations and retain FRED DGS fallback.

evaluation.sourceAuthority: strong: direct publisher is primary for DGS
evaluation.freshness: strong: same business-daily class with validation retained
evaluation.expectedLatency: strong by request shape: one batch covers three rates
evaluation.worstCaseLatency: mixed: bounded Treasury plus sequential fallback
evaluation.timeoutAmplification: strong: one bounded primary rate slot
evaluation.retryAmplification: strong: at most two Treasury attempts
evaluation.cacheHitLatency: strong: provider order is bypassed on hit
evaluation.rateLimitExposure: strong: one Treasury batch before FRED DGS
evaluation.externalDependencyExposure: strong: direct source plus alternate relay
evaluation.migrationRisk: mixed: a pure form would still need repository-specific VIX/cache rules
evaluation.regressionRisk: mixed: implementation changes if applied literally

options.name: FRED-first
options.definition: After cache lookup, attempt FRED VIXCLS DGS10 DGS30 and DGS2 when requested before Treasury fallback.

evaluation.sourceAuthority: mixed: official relay precedes direct DGS publisher
evaluation.freshness: strong: same business-daily class with validation retained
evaluation.expectedLatency: mixed by request shape: multiple sequential calls
evaluation.worstCaseLatency: mixed: FRED sequence plus Treasury fallback
evaluation.retryAmplification: strong: no consumer retry for FRED
evaluation.cacheHitLatency: strong: provider order is bypassed on hit
evaluation.rateLimitExposure: weak: up to three additional FRED DGS calls
evaluation.externalDependencyExposure: mixed: FRED credential and relay lead
evaluation.consumerVisibleTruth: strong if exact source identity is retained
evaluation.regressionRisk: weak: conflicts with current source and timeout contracts

options.name: Race or concurrent
options.definition: Launch Treasury and FRED DGS concurrently and accept a policy-defined winner.

evaluation.sourceAuthority: mixed: timing may choose relay over direct publisher
evaluation.freshness: mixed: winner may vary by timing
evaluation.worstCaseLatency: mixed: bounded race still performs more work
evaluation.retryAmplification: weak: retries can overlap the competing provider
evaluation.cacheEfficiency: mixed: duplicate winner and loser work
evaluation.cacheHitLatency: strong: provider race is bypassed on hit
evaluation.partialFailureBehavior: weak: cancellation and winner arbitration add states
evaluation.providerIsolation: weak: simultaneous egress paths enlarge the boundary
evaluation.noLiveBehavior: mixed: more fanout must be blocked and evidenced
evaluation.observability: weak: winner loser and cancellation telemetry required
evaluation.consumerVisibleTruth: mixed: timing can change selected source
evaluation.migrationRisk: weak: substantial new behavior and tests

options.name: Cache-first bounded order
options.definition: Strengthen cache or prewarm as the main latency policy, then use a bounded provider order selected separately.

evaluation.sourceAuthority: mixed: cached origin varies and cold order is unresolved
evaluation.freshness: strong on a valid hit, unresolved on miss
evaluation.timeoutAmplification: mixed: avoided on hit, unresolved on miss
evaluation.retryAmplification: mixed: avoided on hit, unresolved on miss
evaluation.partialFailureBehavior: weak: cold partial failures remain unresolved
evaluation.providerIsolation: strong on hit, unresolved on miss
evaluation.noLiveBehavior: strong on hit, unresolved on miss
evaluation.deterministicOutput: mixed: depends on cache state and prior source
evaluation.observability: mixed: requires stronger cache and prewarm telemetry
evaluation.operationalComplexity: mixed: prewarm invalidation and lifecycle work
evaluation.consumerVisibleTruth: mixed: stale and cache policy dominate
evaluation.migrationRisk: mixed: changes cache or prewarm policy

options.name: Hybrid current policy
options.definition: Cache-first, FRED VIXCLS, Treasury-primary DGS batch, then sequential FRED fallback for missing DGS and other series.

evaluation.sourceAuthority: strong: direct DGS publisher with exact fallback identity
evaluation.freshness: strong: existing validation and same daily class
evaluation.expectedLatency: strong by request shape: cache VIX then one rate batch
evaluation.timeoutAmplification: strong: Treasury occupies a 0.25 second slot
evaluation.retryAmplification: strong: retries are isolated to the Treasury slot
evaluation.cacheEfficiency: strong: shared micro-cache and panel caches
evaluation.partialFailureBehavior: strong: usable rows skip duplicate fallback
evaluation.noLiveBehavior: strong: injected transport identity stays explicit
evaluation.deterministicOutput: strong: fixed sequence and exact source identity
evaluation.rateLimitExposure: strong: successful Treasury batch skips FRED DGS
evaluation.consumerVisibleTruth: strong: source freshness and availability stay explicit
evaluation.regressionRisk: strong: only stale tests require reconciliation
evaluation.rollbackComplexity: strong: revert the test-only T568 commit

recommendation.policyName: cache-first VIX-first Treasury-primary DGS with FRED DGS fallback
rationale: Treasury is the direct publisher for overlapping DGS observations while FRED is a relay.
One Treasury batch covers three DGS observations and successful rows suppress duplicate FRED calls.
The current sequential deadlines retries caches isolation and truth contracts are already covered by 151 focused passing tests outside the four stale expectations.
History shows that current Treasury-primary behavior was intentionally restored after the FRED-first expectations were introduced.
No live latency telemetry supports a concurrency or FRED-first production change.
normativeRules: fresh panel cache and valid micro-cache entries remain first
VIXCLS remains the first cold provider attempt
Treasury remains primary for missing DGS2 DGS10 DGS30
FRED DGS remains fallback for Treasury-missing invalid or timed-out observations
all current deadlines retries and cache TTLs remain unchanged
source observation freshness availability and isolation truth remain unchanged
T568 changes only the four stale expectations

providerAttemptOrder: fresh consumer panel cache returns immediately
eligible stale panel returns explicitly stale while refresh runs in the background
current early stop when the FRED credential is absent or disabled
Treasury batch for missing DGS2 DGS10 DGS30
remaining FRED series in fixed order while skipping DGS rows supplied by Treasury
completeContract.retryPolicy: Treasury at most two attempts inside one supplied timeout for timeout transport error or empty response; no FRED retry added by the consumer.
completeContract.cacheLookupOrder: consumer fresh panel cache then aggregate per-series 15-second micro-cache then providers
completeContract.cacheWriteOrder: usable non-stale observations to per-series micro-cache under exact source truth; usable projection through the existing in-memory snapshot persisted snapshot and panel-cache owner path
completeContract.cacheReuseRules: rates macro and volatility reuse the same valid observations within 15 seconds; failed empty or stale rows are not stored as observations
completeContract.staleCacheEligibility: eligible prior panel or snapshot only and always explicitly stale; stale provider rows do not enter the official aggregate
completeContract.sourceAuthoritySelection: direct Treasury publisher for overlapping DGS rows; exact FRED relay identity for fallback; FRED authority for VIXCLS and other FRED-only series
completeContract.fallbackEligibility: FRED DGS only when matching Treasury observation is missing invalid stale timed out or otherwise unusable; outer stale-panel fallback remains separate
completeContract.partialResultBehavior: keep each valid observation; missing series remain unavailable and null; successful Treasury subsets suppress only matching FRED DGS calls
completeContract.unavailableProjection: null and unavailable, never zero or neutral
completeContract.staleProjection: explicit stale only from an eligible prior panel or snapshot, never fresh
completeContract.failureReasonProjection: retain allowlisted sanitized not configured cache miss transport error HTTP error timeout missing credential disabled configuration empty response stale official row parse error missing series refresh not attempted or budget exhausted reasons; keep provider-attempt detail when both official paths are attempted
completeContract.noLiveUatBehavior: configuration does not authorize egress; default live transport remains blocked unless allowlisted; injected transport remains fixture mock evidence
completeContract.oneSourceSlow: per-provider caps and the aggregate deadline bound waiting; a slow Treasury slot leaves protected budget for eligible FRED work
completeContract.oneSourceFails: use FRED fallback only for overlapping DGS; keep valid partial observations; FRED-only failures remain unavailable unless an eligible stale panel exists
completeContract.allSourcesFail: synthesize nothing; return explicit unavailable or partial truth, or an eligible prior panel explicitly stale through the outer fallback path
completeContract.protectedNodeTreatment: update all four stale expectations for Treasury-before-FRED DGS bounded Treasury protection and duplicate-call suppression through successful Treasury rows plus micro-cache reuse

explicitlyOutOfScope: changing absent or disabled FRED credential activation behavior
changing topology or known baseline declarations

rejectedAlternatives.reason: Defensible for DGS alone but incomplete for VIX-first and shared-cache policy; Option E is its complete current repository form.
Restores stale expectations at the cost of direct-source precedence and avoidable sequential FRED DGS requests.
No latency telemetry supports doubled egress timing-dependent selection cancellation and new arbitration complexity.
Cache-first is already a policy layer but does not independently decide the cold-miss provider order.

t568Ownership.stopCondition: If Option E requires a production change, stop T568 and request a new decision instead of expanding scope.

t568RequiredAssertions: VIX-first then Treasury-primary DGS order on a cold miss
FRED DGS fallback after Treasury timeout or miss without exceeding the aggregate budget contract
a slow Treasury slot leaves protected budget for remaining FRED work
successful Treasury DGS suppresses matching FRED DGS requests
rates macro and volatility reuse observations inside the micro-cache TTL
exact provider source and freshness truth remains intact

commands.name: boundaries freshness fallback isolation and API focus

validation.warnings: Three existing framework or dependency deprecations; no assertion impact.

uncertainties: No live provider p50 or p95 latency telemetry was available or generated.
No live upstream availability-correlation evidence was generated.
The decision compares request shape and enforced budgets rather than claiming observed production latency.

risks.risk: Changing literal call arrays without preserving behavioral assertions could mask runtime drift.
risks.mitigation: T568 must assert order timeout fallback source and cross-consumer reuse semantics.

risks.risk: The existing FRED credential activation gate may prevent Treasury-only cold fanout.
risks.mitigation: Keep it unchanged in T568 and raise a separate protected-policy decision if product behavior should change.

risks.risk: A concurrency option could change selected source based on timing and double provider exposure.
risks.mitigation: Reject concurrency without telemetry and an explicit authority and cancellation contract.

risks.risk: Test ownership or topology could drift during implementation.
risks.mitigation: Limit T568 to one existing test file and rerun topology verify-all.

releaseRisk: low and test-only after approval if T568 stays within one test file and all focused validation passes; no live-provider qualification claim
rollbackRisk: low because no schema data cache migration or production behavior change is expected; reverting T568 reopens the four known failures

approval.heading: Decision awaiting user approval:
approval.recommendedPolicy: Option E - retain cache-first VIX-first, Treasury-primary DGS, and FRED DGS fallback; update the four stale test expectations only.
approval.proposedCommitSubject: fix(market): decide official macro fetch order
